member.summary: Hybrid Aware allows you to use a distributed cache client but notify any hybrid clients to invalidate their local cache.

member.name: T:Foundatio.Caching.ICacheClient
member.summary: Provides a unified interface for cache operations across different cache implementations. All implementations should behave consistently for the same operations.

remarks.para: Expiration Behavior: The expiresIn parameter controls cache entry expiration:

item.description: null: The entry will not expire (or uses implementation-specific default behavior). When called on an existing key, passing null removes any existing expiration.
Positive value: The entry will expire after the specified duration from now.
Zero or negative value: The operation is treated as expired - any existing key is removed and the method returns a failure/default value (false, 0, depending on the method).
TimeSpan.MaxValue: The entry will not expire (treated as no expiration).

member.name: M:Foundatio.Caching.ICacheClient.RemoveAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Removes a cache entry by its key.
member.param: The unique identifier for the cache entry. Cannot be null or empty.
member.returns: true if the key was found and removed; false if the key did not exist or was already expired.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Caching.ICacheClient.RemoveIfEqualAsync``1(System.String,``0)
member.summary: Removes a cache entry only if its current value equals the expected value. This operation is atomic and can be used for optimistic concurrency control.
member.typeparam: The type of the cached value.
member.param: The unique identifier for the cache entry. Cannot be null or empty.
The expected value that must match the current cached value for removal to succeed.
member.returns: true if the key existed, the value matched, and the entry was removed; false if the key did not exist or the value did not match.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Caching.ICacheClient.RemoveAllAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String})
member.summary: Removes multiple cache entries, or all entries if no keys are specified.
member.param: The keys to remove. If null or empty, all cache entries will be removed (flush). Each key in the collection cannot be null or empty.
member.returns: The number of entries that were removed.
member.exception: Thrown when any key in is null or empty.

member.summary: Removes all cache entries whose keys start with the specified prefix.
member.param: The prefix to match. If null or empty, all cache entries will be removed (flush).
member.returns: The number of entries that were removed.

member.param: The unique identifier for the cache entry. Cannot be null or empty.

member.summary: Atomically increments a floating-point value stored at the specified key. If the key does not exist, it is initialized to .
member.param: The unique identifier for the cache entry. Cannot be null or empty.
The amount to increment by. Can be positive, negative (decrement), or zero. Supports fractional values (e.g., 1.5, -0.25).

null: Removes any existing expiration. For new keys, no expiration is set.
Positive value: Sets or updates expiration to this duration from now.

Zero or negative: Any existing key is removed, returns 0.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Caching.ICacheClient.ListAddAsync``1(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan})
member.summary: Adds values to a list stored at the specified key. If the key does not exist, a new list is created.
member.typeparam: The type of values in the list.
member.param: The unique identifier for the cache entry. Cannot be null or empty.
The values to add to the list. Cannot be null. Null values within the collection are ignored.

Optional expiration time for each value being added (per-value expiration, NOT per-key).
null: Values will not expire.
Positive value: Each value expires independently after this duration.
Zero or negative: The specified values are removed from the list if present, returns 0.
Design Note: Per-value expiration prevents unbounded list growth. Without it, adding any item would reset the entire list's TTL (sliding expiration), causing lists to grow indefinitely in scenarios like tracking deleted items or recent activity.
member.returns: The number of values that were added to the list.
member.remarks: The key's overall expiration is automatically set to the maximum expiration of all items in the list.

member.returns: The number of values that were removed from the list.

The number of items per page when paginating. Defaults to 100.
member.returns: A containing the list values if found. Use to check if the list exists.

member.name: T:Foundatio.Caching.IMemoryCacheClient
member.summary: Marker interface for in-memory cache implementations. Used to identify caches that store data in process memory rather than external stores.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Caching.InMemoryCacheClient.UpdateMemorySize(System.Int64)
member.summary: Safely updates the current memory size, ensuring it never goes negative and handles overflow.
member.remarks: This method is a no-op when memory tracking is disabled or when the delta is zero.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Caching.InMemoryCacheClient.UpdateMemorySizeForEntry(System.Int64,System.Int64,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Updates memory size when an entry is added or updated. Calculates the size delta based on old and new entry sizes.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Caching.InMemoryCacheClient.RecalculateMemorySize
member.summary: Recalculates the current memory size by summing all non-expired cache entries.

remarks.para: Note: This method iterates over the cache dictionary which may be modified concurrently. The calculated total may be temporarily inaccurate if entries are added/removed during iteration, but this is acceptable for memory tracking purposes and will self-correct on subsequent operations or recalculations.

Known limitation: If an entry's value is updated during iteration (via the Value setter which resets the estimated size), the calculation could include a mix of old and newly-calculated sizes. This is acceptable for approximate memory tracking. To reduce this window, a snapshot of values is taken at the start of the iteration.
Expired entries are excluded from the calculation to ensure accurate memory reporting.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Caching.InMemoryCacheClient.RemoveExpiredKey(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Removes cache entry from expires in argument value.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Caching.InMemoryCacheClient.RemoveKeyIfExpired(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Used by the maintenance task to remove expired keys.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Caching.InMemoryCacheClient.FindWorstSizeToUsageRatio
member.summary: Finds the cache entry with the worst size-to-usage ratio for eviction.

remarks.para: This method implements a "waste score" algorithm that considers multiple factors:

list.item: Size: Larger entries receive higher scores (logarithmic scale in KB)
Age: Older entries receive slightly higher scores (0.5x weight)
Access recency: Less recently accessed entries receive higher scores (2x weight)

remarks.para: The algorithm prioritizes evicting entries that consume more memory and haven't been accessed recently. In uniform access patterns (all entries accessed equally), the algorithm may evict entries based primarily on size and age, which means recently-accessed entries could still be evicted if they are large and old. This is intentional behavior to optimize memory usage over strict LRU ordering.
Expired entries are always prioritized and returned immediately if found.

Performance note: This method performs an O(n) iteration over all cache entries on each eviction. For very large caches (100k+ entries), this could become a bottleneck during high-contention compaction. However, because eviction typically removes multiple items per compaction cycle (scaled by maxRemovals) and compaction is throttled to run infrequently, this trade-off favors simplicity and correctness over more complex data structures like priority queues that would add memory overhead and complexity for all cache operations. For extremely large caches with frequent eviction, consider using to bound the cache size at a level where O(n) iteration remains acceptable.

member.returns: The key of the entry to evict, or null if no suitable candidate found.

member.summary: Calculates the size of a cache entry. Returns -1 if the entry should not be cached (exceeds MaxEntrySize or invalid size from calculator). Returns 0 immediately if no size calculator is configured.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Caching.InMemoryCacheClientOptions.SizeCalculator
member.summary: Function to calculate the size of cache entries in bytes. Required when or is set.

remarks.para: Entry sizing is opt-in for performance. When or is set, a must also be provided.
Use for automatic size calculation using , or for maximum performance with uniform entry sizes.

list.item: Must return a non-negative value representing the estimated size in bytes
Negative return values will cause the entry to be skipped (not cached) with a warning logged
Should be thread-safe as it may be called concurrently
Should handle null values gracefully (typically return 8 bytes for a null reference)

member.summary: The maximum size in bytes for individual cache entries. Entries larger than this will be skipped (not cached) and a warning will be logged. If null, no entry size limit is enforced. Must be less than or equal to when both are set.

member.name: F:Foundatio.Caching.CacheClientExtensions.MinimumExpiration
member.summary: Minimum meaningful cache expiration. Values below this threshold are treated as already-expired because sub-millisecond TTLs are truncated to zero by external providers (e.g., Redis PSETEX converts TimeSpan to milliseconds via integer cast, so 0.9ms becomes 0ms and is rejected). 5ms provides a safe margin above the 1ms integer-truncation boundary while remaining far below any real-world cache TTL.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Caching.CacheClientExtensions.ToExpiresIn(Foundatio.Caching.ICacheClient,System.Nullable{System.DateTime})
member.summary: Converts a DateTime expiration to a TimeSpan relative to now. DateTime.MaxValue is treated as null (no expiration). Returns TimeSpan.Zero when the computed TTL is below , so downstream guards treat it as already-expired.

member.name: T:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator
member.summary: Calculates the estimated memory size of objects. Uses fast paths for common types and falls back to JSON serialization for complex objects.
member.remarks:
The type size cache is bounded to prevent unbounded memory growth. When the cache reaches its limit, older entries are evicted. Call to clear the cache when the calculator is no longer needed.
Note: For collections implementing , size estimation samples the first 50 items and extrapolates based on the collection's total item count. For other types, only the sampled items are measured without extrapolation. In both cases, estimates may underestimate size if unsampled later items are significantly larger.

member.name: F:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator.DefaultMaxTypeCacheSize
member.summary: Default maximum number of types to cache size calculations for.

member.name: F:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator.ReferenceSize
member.summary: Size of a null reference in bytes.

member.name: F:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator.StringOverhead
member.summary: Base overhead for string objects (object header + length field + null terminator padding).

member.name: F:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator.ArrayOverhead
member.summary: Base overhead for array objects (object header + length + type pointer).

member.name: F:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator.CollectionOverhead
member.summary: Base overhead for collection objects (object header + internal array reference + count + version).

member.name: F:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator.ObjectOverhead
member.summary: Base overhead for complex objects (object header + method table pointer).

member.name: F:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator.DefaultObjectSize
member.summary: Default size estimate when type size cannot be determined.

member.name: F:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator.CollectionSampleLimit
member.summary: Maximum number of collection items to sample for size estimation. Only the first N items are measured; the average is extrapolated to the full collection.

member.name: F:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator.MaxRecursionDepth
member.summary: Maximum recursion depth for nested collection size calculation to prevent stack overflow.

member.name: F:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator.EvictionPercentage
member.summary: Maximum percentage of cache entries to evict at once (10%).

member.name: F:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator.MaxEvictionCount
member.summary: Maximum number of entries to evict in a single eviction pass.

member.name: F:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator.TypeSizeLookupTable
member.summary: Static lookup table for primitive and common value type sizes. Uses FrozenDictionary for optimal read performance.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggerFactory)
member.summary: Creates a new SizeCalculator instance with default settings.
member.param: Optional logger factory for diagnostic logging.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator.#ctor(System.Int32,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggerFactory)
member.summary: Creates a new SizeCalculator instance.
member.param: Maximum number of types to cache size calculations for. Default is 1000.
Optional logger factory for diagnostic logging.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator.TypeCacheCount
member.summary: Gets the current number of types in the size cache.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator.Dispose
member.summary: Clears the type size cache and releases resources.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Utility.SizeCalculator.CalculateSize(System.Object)
member.summary: Calculates the estimated memory size of an object in bytes.
member.param: The object to calculate size for.
member.returns: Estimated size in bytes.

remarks.para: For primitive types passed as object, this method returns the raw value size (e.g., 4 bytes for int, 8 bytes for long) rather than the full boxed object size. Boxed primitives in .NET actually consume more memory (typically 12-24 bytes due to object header overhead), but this calculator prioritizes performance over absolute accuracy for primitive types. The returned sizes are useful for relative comparisons and capacity planning.

member.exception: Thrown if the calculator has been disposed.

member.summary: Cache entry for storing type size calculations with LRU tracking. Uses a mutable field for LastAccess to support Interlocked operations.

member.summary: Cache entry for storing type size calculations with LRU tracking. Uses a mutable field for LastAccess to support Interlocked operations.

member.summary: Mutable field for thread-safe LRU tracking via Interlocked operations. Initialized from InitialLastAccess in the primary constructor.

member.summary: Specifies the cloning behavior for a member (field, property, event) or an entire type (class, struct, record). When applied to a type, all members of that type will use this behavior unless overridden at the member level. This is the base attribute for configuring clone behavior.

member.name: T:Foundatio.FastCloner.Code.FastClonerIgnoreAttribute
member.summary: Marks a member or type as ignored, effectively assigning a default value when cloning. When applied to a type, all usages of that type will be set to default. When applied to a member, that specific member will be set to default.
member.remarks: This attribute is a shorthand for [FastClonerBehavior(CloneBehavior.Ignore)].

member.name: M:Foundatio.FastCloner.Code.FastClonerIgnoreAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of .
member.param: Whether the member/type should be ignored during cloning. Default is true.

member.name: P:Foundatio.FastCloner.Code.FastClonerIgnoreAttribute.Ignored
member.summary: Gets whether the member/type should be ignored during cloning.

member.name: T:Foundatio.FastCloner.Code.FastClonerReferenceAttribute
member.summary: Marks a member or type to preserve the original reference during cloning. When applied to a type, all usages of that type will share the same reference. When applied to a member, that specific member will share the same reference. This is useful for shared services, singletons, or immutable objects that should not be duplicated.
member.remarks: This attribute is a shorthand for [FastClonerBehavior(CloneBehavior.Reference)].

member.name: M:Foundatio.FastCloner.Code.FastClonerReferenceAttribute.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of .

member.name: T:Foundatio.FastCloner.Code.FastClonerSafeTypes
member.summary: Safe types are types, which can be copied without real cloning. e.g. simple structs or strings (it is immutable)

member.name: M:Foundatio.FastCloner.Code.FastClonerSafeTypes.HasStableHashSemantics(System.Type)
member.summary: Determines whether GetHashCode() result won't change after deep cloning (best effort).

member.name: T:Foundatio.FastCloner.Code.FastClonerShallowAttribute
member.summary: Marks a member or type for shallow cloning instead of deep cloning. When applied to a type, all usages perform MemberwiseClone without recursing into members. When applied to a member, that reference is copied directly without deep cloning its contents. This is useful for parent references, shared state, or when deep cloning would cause issues.
member.remarks: This attribute is a shorthand for [FastClonerBehavior(CloneBehavior.Shallow)].

member.name: M:Foundatio.FastCloner.Code.FastClonerShallowAttribute.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of .

member.name: T:Foundatio.FastCloner.FastClonerSafeHandleAttribute
member.summary: Apply this attribute to a struct to indicate it should be treated as a "Safe Handle" or identity-preserving type. When applied, FastCloner will NOT attempt to deep-clone readonly fields on this struct, preventing identity breakage for types that rely on specific internal singleton or handle references.

member.name: T:Foundatio.Lock.ILockProvider
member.summary: Provides distributed locking to coordinate access to shared resources across processes.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Lock.ILockProvider.AcquireAsync(System.String,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Boolean,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Acquires a lock on the specified resource. Throws if the lock cannot be obtained before the supplied is cancelled.
member.param: The resource identifier to lock. Use consistent naming across processes.
How long the lock is held before automatic release. Defaults to 20 minutes. For long-running operations, call periodically.
If true, the lock is released when disposed.

Token used to abort the acquisition attempt. Pass a token from a with a CancelAfter to apply a timeout. The method throws when the token is cancelled before acquisition completes. An already-cancelled token is treated as an immediate-deadline attempt: a single acquire is tried and is thrown if it fails.
member.returns: The acquired .
member.exception: The lock could not be acquired before cancellation.
member.remarks: Use this when the work cannot run safely without the lock. For best-effort acquisition where failure is a normal control-flow outcome, call and check the result for null.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Lock.ILockProvider.TryAcquireAsync(System.String,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Boolean,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Attempts to acquire a lock on the specified resource. Returns null if the lock cannot be obtained before the supplied is cancelled.
member.param: The resource identifier to lock. Use consistent naming across processes.
How long the lock is held before automatic release. Defaults to 20 minutes. For long-running operations, call periodically.
If true, the lock is released when disposed.

Token used to abort the acquisition attempt. Pass a token from a with a CancelAfter to apply a timeout. When acquisition cannot complete before cancellation, the method returns null rather than throwing. An already-cancelled token is treated as an immediate-deadline attempt: a single acquire is tried and null is returned if it fails.
member.returns: The acquired , or null if the lock could not be obtained.
member.remarks: Use this when lock unavailability is a normal control-flow outcome (best-effort dedupe, opportunistic work). When the work cannot run without the lock, call instead.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Lock.ILockProvider.IsLockedAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Checks whether a resource is currently locked.
member.param: The resource identifier to check.
member.returns: True if the resource is locked; otherwise, false.

The unique identifier of the lock to release.

member.name: T:Foundatio.Messaging.IMessageBus
member.summary: Represents a message bus that supports both publishing and subscribing to messages.

member.name: T:Foundatio.Messaging.MessageOptions
member.summary: Options for configuring message publishing behavior.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.MessageOptions.UniqueId
member.summary: Gets or sets a unique identifier for the message. Can be used for message deduplication and idempotency checks.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.MessageOptions.CorrelationId
member.summary: Gets or sets the correlation identifier for distributed tracing. If not set, this is automatically populated from when the message is published, enabling end-to-end request tracing across services.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.MessageOptions.DeliveryDelay
member.summary:
Gets or sets the delay before the message is delivered to subscribers.
Support for delayed delivery varies by provider. Some providers support native delayed delivery where messages are persisted and survive application restarts. Other providers use an in-memory fallback where messages are held in memory until the delay expires.
Warning: For providers using the in-memory fallback, delayed messages are not persisted and will be lost if the application restarts before the delay expires. Check your provider's documentation for specific behavior.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.MessageOptions.Properties
member.summary: Gets or sets custom properties to include with the message. These properties are propagated through the message bus and available to subscribers. The mechanism for propagation varies by provider implementation.

member.name: T:Foundatio.Messaging.IMessagePublisher

member.param: The type used to route the message to subscribers.
The message payload to publish.
Optional settings for delivery delay, correlation ID, and custom properties.
Token to cancel the publish operation.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.MessageBusBase`1.DisposedCancellationToken
member.summary: Gets a cancellation token that is canceled when this instance is disposed. Use this token to cancel background operations during shutdown.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.MessageBusBase`1.GetLinkedDisposableCancellationTokenSource(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Creates a linked cancellation token source that combines the provided token with the disposal token. This allows operations to be cancelled by either the caller or when this instance is disposed.
member.param: The caller's cancellation token to link.
member.returns: A new that should be disposed by the caller.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.MessageBusBase`1.EnsureTopicCreatedAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Called before publishing to ensure the topic exists. The is always ; topic creation should only abort when the message bus is being disposed, never due to an individual caller's cancellation.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.MessageBusBase`1.ShutdownAsync
member.summary: Called during the first phase of disposal, before is cancelled and before subscribers are cleared.

remarks.para: Override this method to gracefully drain in-flight work (stop processors, close consumer groups, flush buffers). Subscribers are still registered and the cancellation token is still active, so handlers can finish processing normally.
The base implementation delegates to so that existing provider overrides continue to work without changes.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.MessageBusBase`1.CleanupAsync
member.summary: Called during the second phase of disposal, after is cancelled and after subscribers are cleared.
member.remarks: Override this method to tear down transport infrastructure — close connections, dispose clients, await background listener tasks. No subscribers remain at this point and the cancellation token has been signaled, so background loops should have exited.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.MessageBusBase`1.RemoveTopicSubscriptionAsync
member.summary: Called during the first phase of disposal to remove transport-level topic subscriptions. New providers should prefer overriding instead, which calls this method by default.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.MessageBusBase`1.EnsureTopicSubscriptionAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Called after subscribing to ensure the topic subscription infrastructure exists. The is always ; subscription setup should only abort when the message bus is being disposed, never due to an individual caller's cancellation.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.MessageBusBase`1.SendDelayedMessage(System.Type,System.Object,Foundatio.Messaging.MessageOptions)
member.summary: Schedules a message for delayed delivery using an in-memory timer.
member.remarks: This method calls (not ) to ensure topic infrastructure is re-established if needed (e.g., after reconnection for external providers). The is cleared via record copy to prevent infinite recursion. The Properties dictionary is cloned to avoid shared mutable state between the caller and delayed delivery.

member.name: T:Foundatio.AsyncEx.AsyncLock
member.summary: A mutual exclusion lock that is compatible with async. Note that this lock is not recursive!

remarks.para: This is the async-ready almost-equivalent of the lock keyword or the type, similar to Stephen Toub's AsyncLock. It's only almost equivalent because the lock keyword permits reentrancy, which is not currently possible to do with an async-ready lock.

An is either taken or not. The lock can be asynchronously acquired by calling , and it is released by disposing the result of that task. takes an optional , which can be used to cancel the acquiring of the lock.

The task returned from will enter the Completed state when it has acquired the . That same task will enter the Canceled state if the is signaled before the wait is satisfied; in that case, the is not taken by that task.
You can call or with an already-cancelled to attempt to acquire the immediately without actually entering the wait queue.

example.para: The vast majority of use cases are to just replace a lock statement. That is, with the original code looking like this:
If we want to replace the blocking operation Thread.Sleep with an asynchronous equivalent, it's not directly possible because of the lock block. We cannot await inside of a lock.
So, we use the async-compatible instead:

member.summary: A base class for disposables that need exactly-once semantics in a thread-safe way. All disposals of this instance block until the disposal is complete.
member.typeparam: The type of "context" for the derived disposable. Since the context should not be modified, strongly consider making this an immutable type.

remarks.para: If is called multiple times, only the first call will execute the disposal code. Other calls to will wait for the disposal to complete.

member.summary: Whether this instance is disposed (finished disposing).

member.summary: Whether this instance is currently disposing, but not finished yet.

member.summary: Automatically detects and discards duplicate entries in a queue based on a unique identifier.

member.name: T:Foundatio.Queues.IQueue`1
member.summary: A typed message queue that supports enqueue, dequeue, and work item lifecycle management. Entries are processed with at-least-once delivery semantics and must be explicitly completed or abandoned.
member.typeparam: The type of message payload stored in the queue.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Queues.IQueue`1.Enqueuing
member.summary: Raised before an item is enqueued. Set to prevent enqueueing.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Queues.IQueue`1.Enqueued
member.summary: Raised after an item has been successfully enqueued.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Queues.IQueue`1.Dequeued
member.summary: Raised after an item has been dequeued and is ready for processing.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Queues.IQueue`1.LockRenewed
member.summary: Raised after a queue entry's lock has been renewed.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Queues.IQueue`1.Completed
member.summary: Raised after a queue entry has been marked as completed.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Queues.IQueue`1.Abandoned
member.summary: Raised after a queue entry has been abandoned and returned to the queue. When a poison message (deserialization failure) is detected, the entry is automatically abandoned and this event is raised with a phantom entry whose is null at runtime. See for details.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Queues.IQueue`1.QueueDeleted
member.summary: Raised after the queue has been deleted.

member.summary: Attaches a behavior that can intercept and modify queue operations.
member.param: The behavior to attach.

member.summary: Adds an item to the queue for processing.

Optional settings for delivery delay, correlation ID, and custom properties.
member.returns: The unique identifier assigned to the queued entry.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Queues.IQueue`1.DequeueAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Retrieves and locks the next available item from the queue. Blocks until an item is available or the cancellation token is triggered.
member.param: Token to cancel the wait for an item.
member.returns: The dequeued entry, or null if cancelled before an item became available.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Queues.IQueue`1.DequeueAsync(System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan})
member.summary: Retrieves and locks the next available item from the queue.
member.param: Maximum time to wait for an item. Defaults to 30 seconds.
member.returns: The dequeued entry, or null if no item was available within the timeout.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Queues.IQueue`1.RenewLockAsync(Foundatio.Queues.IQueueEntry{`0})
member.summary: Extends the processing lock on a queue entry to prevent it from being redelivered. Call periodically for long-running work items.
member.param: The entry whose lock should be renewed.

member.summary: Marks a queue entry as successfully processed and removes it from the queue.
member.param: The entry to complete.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Queues.IQueue`1.AbandonAsync(Foundatio.Queues.IQueueEntry{`0})
member.summary: Returns a queue entry to the queue for reprocessing. The entry will be redelivered after a delay, up to the maximum retry limit.

member.summary: Retrieves items that have exceeded the maximum retry attempts.
member.param: Token to cancel the operation.
member.returns: The collection of dead-lettered message payloads.

member.summary: Starts a background worker that continuously dequeues and processes items.
member.param: The async function invoked for each dequeued entry.

Token to stop the background worker.

member.name: T:Foundatio.Queues.AbandonedEventArgs`1
member.summary: Event arguments for the event.
member.typeparam: The type of message payload.
member.remarks: When a queue entry is abandoned due to a deserialization failure (poison message), . may be null at runtime even though the property is typed as non-nullable. This occurs because the message payload could not be deserialized, so the queue creates a phantom entry with a null value that is immediately abandoned. Handlers subscribing to the event should check for null before accessing Value if they need the payload.

member.summary: The queue raising the event.

member.summary: Gets the CLR type of the message payload.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Queues.IQueueEntry.GetValue
member.summary: Gets the message payload as an untyped object.
member.remarks: For poison messages (deserialization failures), the return value is null at runtime even though the signature is non-nullable. Poison entries are immediately abandoned and only observable via the event.

member.summary: Gets whether this entry has been marked as completed.

member.summary: Marks this entry as abandoned locally without notifying the queue. Use to return the entry to the queue for reprocessing.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Queues.IQueueEntry.MarkCompleted
member.summary: Marks this entry as completed locally without notifying the queue. Use to remove the entry from the queue.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Queues.IQueueEntry.RenewLockAsync
member.summary: Extends the processing lock to prevent the entry from being redelivered. Call periodically for long-running work items.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Queues.IQueueEntry.AbandonAsync
member.summary: Returns this entry to the queue for reprocessing. The entry will be redelivered after a delay, up to the maximum retry limit.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Queues.IQueueEntry.CompleteAsync
member.summary: Marks this entry as successfully processed and removes it from the queue.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Queues.IQueueEntry.DisposeAsync
member.summary: Releases resources associated with this entry. If not completed or abandoned, the entry will be automatically abandoned.

member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum number of calls that must be made during the sampling period before the circuit breaker can open. Default is 100.

member.summary: Circuit is manually opened and no attempts are allowed

member.name: T:Foundatio.Resilience.ICircuitBreaker
member.summary: Implements the circuit breaker pattern to prevent cascading failures. When failures exceed a threshold, the circuit "opens" to fail fast and allow the system to recover.
member.remarks:
Circuit states:
Closed: Normal operation, calls are allowed.
Open: Failures exceeded threshold, calls fail immediately.
HalfOpen: Testing recovery, limited calls allowed.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Resilience.ICircuitBreaker.State
member.summary: Gets the current state of the circuit breaker.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Resilience.ICircuitBreaker.BeforeCall
member.summary: Called before executing a protected operation. Throws if the circuit is open and calls should not be attempted.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Resilience.ICircuitBreaker.RecordCallSuccess
member.summary: Records a successful call, potentially closing the circuit if in half-open state.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Resilience.ICircuitBreaker.RecordCallFailure(System.Exception)
member.summary: Records a failed call, potentially opening the circuit if failures exceed the threshold.
member.param: The exception that caused the failure.

member.summary: Gets or sets the logger for this policy.

member.summary: The maximum number of attempts to execute the action. Default is 3 attempts.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Resilience.ResiliencePolicy.UnhandledExceptions
member.summary: A collection of exception types that will not be handled by the policy. These exceptions will be thrown immediately without retrying. Default includes OperationCanceledException and BrokenCircuitException.

member.summary: A function that determines whether to retry based on the attempt number and exception.

member.summary: Sets a fixed retry delay for all retries.

member.summary: Gets or sets a function that returns the retry delay based on the number of attempts. Default is an exponential delay starting at 1 second.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Resilience.ResiliencePolicy.MaxDelay
member.summary: Sets the max retry delay for all retries. Default is null, meaning no maximum delay.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Resilience.ResiliencePolicy.UseJitter
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to use jitter in the backoff interval. Default is false.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Resilience.ResiliencePolicy.Timeout
member.summary: Gets or sets the timeout for the entire operation.

member.summary: Marker interface for serializers that produce human-readable text output (e.g., JSON, XML). Text serializers use UTF-8 encoding for string conversions in extension methods.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Serializer.SerializerExtensions.Deserialize``1(Foundatio.Serializer.ISerializer,System.IO.Stream)
member.summary: Deserializes an object of type from .
member.returns: The deserialized value, or default if the underlying serializer returns null.
member.remarks: The return type is T annotated with [return: MaybeNull] rather than T? because T? on an unconstrained generic would double-wrap Nullable<T> value types. Callers that expect null should use a nullable type argument, e.g. Deserialize<MyType?>.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Storage.FileStorageExtensions.GetObjectAsync``1(Foundatio.Storage.IFileStorage,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Deserializes an object of type from the file at . Returns default if the file does not exist.

remarks.para: This method can return null/default when the file is not found, but the return type is Task<T> rather than Task<T?> for two reasons:

item.description: [return: MaybeNull] is not honored by Roslyn inside async methods (dotnet/roslyn#30953), so applying it causes spurious CS8602 warnings at every call site.
Using Task<T?> would double-wrap Nullable<T> value types.

remarks.para: Callers that need null safety should use a nullable type argument, e.g. GetObjectAsync<PostInfo?>(path).

member.name: T:Foundatio.Storage.ScopedFileStorage
member.summary: Provides a scoped file storage implementation that prefixes all file paths with the specified scope. Can optionally dispose the underlying storage when this instance is disposed.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Storage.ScopedFileStorage.#ctor(Foundatio.Storage.IFileStorage,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified file storage and scope.
member.param: The underlying file storage to use.
The scope for file paths. When specified, all operations will be prefixed with this scope.

Whether to dispose the underlying file storage when this instance is disposed. Defaults to false, meaning the underlying file storage will not be disposed when this instance is disposed. Set to true to have the underlying file storage automatically disposed when this instance is disposed, enabling use with 'using' statements.
member.exception: Thrown when contains a wildcard character.
